cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is about a passage that discusses the ownership of the "Happy Birthday to You" song. The user wants to know what the history experts' statement proves about the song. First, I need to look at the passage again. The main points are: - Filmmaker Jennifer Nelson had to pay $1500 to use the song in her documentary about its history. - Warner Music Group claims copyright since 1988. - Nelson's lawyers argue that the song belongs to the public because of its history. - The song was originally written by the Hill sisters as "Good Morning to All" in 1893. - Over time, people started using the "Happy Birthday"
